sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.103 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Is there a rank analogue of the Leedham-Green--McKay--Shepherd theorem on$p$-groups of maximal class? More precisely, suppose that$P$is a 2-generator finite$p$-group whose lower central quotients$_i(P)/ _{i+1}(P)$are cyclic for all$i 2$. Is it true that$P$contains a normal subgroup$N$of nilpotency class$2$such that the rank of$P/N$is bounded in terms of$p$only? { E.\,I.\,Khukhro} No, moreover, the...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
